Every modern Hollywood film now ends with the protagonist triumphing through sheer self-belief—no training, no preparation, just abstract conviction. Once noticed, it becomes impossible to ignore. It’s formulaic delusion dressed as inspiration.

This narrative crutch has subtly conditioned entire generations to internalize the idea that belief alone is tantamount to ability—that if something feels true, it must be. It’s a seductive fantasy, but dangerously unrealistic. Reality doesn’t bend to sentiment; it yields only to discipline, resilience, and skill.

The greatest action films endure because they respect this principle. The Dark Knight, Bourne, Terminator, Planet of the Apes (Matt Reeves)—these stories are grounded, their worlds coherent, their stakes tangible. The protagonists are not sanctified by wishful thinking but forged through adversity. They are competent, fallible, and fully accountable.

These films resonate because they reflect a reality where triumph must be earned, not imagined. Hollywood has abandoned that ethos. Culture, unsurprisingly, followed.

I have been eagerly awaiting the continuation of the series "Mr. Robot (Elliot Alderson)", but there hasn't been any further information from the director, Sam Esmail. I’m extremely excited about this series.

Thanks to the incredible quality of the "Mr. Robot" sequels from Seasons 1 to 4, I developed a strong motivation to deeply explore Cybercrime and Penetration Testing. It was an intense experience—I once spent three days and nights without sleep, fully immersed in the Linux Ubuntu terminal, conducting various tests with tools from Kali Linux (BackTrack).

I became completely obsessed, to the point where I lost count of how many times I caused computers to crash into BSOD and had to reinstall operating systems. My journey included exploring Advanced Cracking, Vbox, Qemu, Msf4, building compilers, managing system administration, and much more.

saya menunggu kelanjutan film seri "MR ROBOT (ELLIOT ALDERSON)" tetapi sutradara Sam Esmail tidak ada informasi lebih lanjut. Saya sangat menantikan film ini. 

mengingat Sequel MR ROBOT Season 1 - 4 adalah yang terbaik, berkat sequel tersebut saya memiliki motivasi tinggi untuk mendalami Cyber Crime dan Penetration Testing . itu sangat gila, saya pernah 3 hari 3 malam tidak tidur dan hanya berkutat pada terminal Linux Ubuntu dan melakuan pengujian di berbagai alat Kali Linux (BackTrack)

saya sangat amat tergila - gila dan entah sudah berapa kali saya membuat perangkat komputer BSOD, dan melakuan Installasi ulang sistem operasi, Termasuk Advance Cracking, Vbox, Qemu, Msf4, membangun Compillers, System Administrator dan lain sebaginya.

Думаю, тебе нужно это увидеть.
— Что это?
— Твою жену убили.

То, что ты делаешь, очень важно для нас. Позволь нам разобраться с этим.
— Я хочу пройти специальную подготовку, получить деньги и новую личность.
— Ты что, с ума сошёл?
— Я хочу найти и убить людей, которые убили мою жену.
— Не думаю, что ты смог бы победить 90-летнюю монахиню в армрестлинге.
— Я хочу встретиться лицом к лицу с убийцами моей жены, посмотреть им в глаза и восстановить справедливость.

Возьми это. Направь на меня. Сделай это сейчас. Положи палец на курок. Ты не сможешь сделать то, что делаю я. Как бы я тебя ни учил, ты просто не убийца, Чарли. Ты просто ботаник, который работает с компьютерами. Но то, что ты делаешь, тебя убьёт. Ты когда-нибудь задумывался, в чём я хорош?

Ты приставил пистолет к голове моей жены. Это пульт дистанционного управления устройством, которое сейчас разрушает стекло под тобой. Ты ошибся человеком.
— Нет. Я выбрал именно того, кого нужно.

У нас есть зацепка в Риме. Он только что сел в автобус в Праге. Он практически изобрёл половину нашего оборудования для наблюдения.
— Может, вы недооцениваете этого человека?
— Это нужно прекратить.

Ты её узнаёшь? Если ты очень быстро прыгнешь, то, возможно, выживешь при взрыве.
— Точно.

Two consecutive "Stop the CCP’s Live Organ Harvesting Poster Exhibitions" were successfully held in Higashi-hiroshima City Museum of Art and Nishi Ward Culture Center in West Hiroshima from July 25 to August 6, 2023.

The exhibitions were hosted by the SMG Network (Stop Medical Genocide--Society for the Study of Organ Transplantation in China) and exposed the Chinese Communist Party’s (CCP) live organ harvesting from Falun Dafa practitioners. 

Many visitors commented that they had asked themselves the question, “If one's life or the life of a loved one was in danger, would you buy an organ for transplant?” After seeing the poster exhibition visitors said their answer was “absolutely not.”


Stop the CCP’s Live Organ Harvesting Poster Exhibition held in Hiroshima from July 25 to August 6, 2023.

Insights Gained After Viewing the Poster Exhibition

A woman in her 60’s told a story about a doctor suggesting her husband get an organ transplant.

A woman in her 60’s told a story that her husband’s doctor had suggested her husband undergo an organ transplant six years ago. If the operation was performed within a week, it would cost 20 million yen by going abroad. If he could wait six months, it would only cost 6 million yen and could be done in Japan. By then, her husband didn’t want to use another person’s organ to prolong his life and didn’t do the surgery. “At that time, I didn’t think about the organ donor at all. Although I have regrets for my husband, seeing these works today, I am very glad that he didn’t have a transplant operation at that time,” she said.


Ms. Den Kazumi 

Ms. Den Kazumi said in shock that she had heard of live organ harvesting, but she didn’t know why or how it happened. She had now connected the dots and said, “I know that many Falun Gong practitioners have been arrested, but are there some who were arrested for organ harvesting?” 

She said the poster The Harvest – Blood & Greed showed that to some people money weighs more than a heart and a liver.

“A person who is struggling to live might need an organ, but if he knows how it was obtained, would he feel right about getting the transplant?” she asked.


College student Yamaoka (right)

Before seeing the poster exhibition, college student Yamaoka wasn’t sure he could say no if one of his family members or someone else close to him could benefit from an organ transplant. “I didn’t have the confidence to say no,” he said. “However, now that I have learned about the victims behind the life-saving organ transplantation [that the CCP harvests organs from living Falun Gong practitioners], I have the confidence to say absolutely not!”
